nodes.label: AI as productivity enhancer
nodes.label_negative: AI as time sink for complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: the greatest example of the power of AI as a productivity tool

nodes.label: AI as practical problem solver
nodes.grounding_span: I have developed the habit of turning to AI as a first port of call

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: performance rapidly degrades with the complexity of the task

nodes.label: Confidentiality in AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I cannot share code with any AI tool for privacy reasons

nodes.label: AI as magic wand for peripheral tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I just wanted the app as if waving a magic wand

nodes.label: Personal autonomy and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: not wanting to see my own skills fade too much

nodes.label: Seamless task completion for non-core work
nodes.grounding_span: I just wanted the app as if waving a magic wand

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for code generation
nodes.grounding_span: to start with a complex paper and end up with it translated into code in one day was extraordinary

nodes.label: Indifferent stance toward AI as non-essential
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes it is extraordinarily useful, sometimes not so much

nodes.label: Negative view of AI for specific recent research
nodes.grounding_span: the time debugging the AI provided code is often just as long as building it from scratch

nodes.label: Neutral expectation toward AI in app development
nodes.grounding_span: I had to dig in myself and google lots of things, when I just wanted the app as if waving a magic wand

nodes.label: Iterative back-and-forth prompting
nodes.grounding_span: chatting back and forth with chatGPT come up with the code

nodes.label: Experience-driven pragmatic calibration of tool use
nodes.grounding_span: It seems to be an art to prompt models correctly and to understand their limitations
